page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg7.xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor running at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It operates on the x86_64 architecture and includes 33 MB of L3 cache, alongside support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AVX512_BF16. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate moderate single-core capabilities but relatively constrained multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieves average ratings in single-core stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests. However, multi-core benchmarks show weaker results, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 2941.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 4151.89. Memory bandwidth is also on the lower end, measuring 31.07 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 25.1 GB/sec for write operations. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression perform at average levels, while multi-threaded decompression and database operations are weak.

From a resource density perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation provides predictable compute performance, though the overall multi-core throughput is modest for its hardware class. Given the lack of local storage and the 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, this instance is best suited for workloads that do not require high I/O performance or massive parallel processing. Appropriate workloads include lightweight web serving, basic text processing, single-threaded application tasks, and general-purpose development or testing environments.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg7.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor at 3.3 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with KVM virtualization, it provides 4.0 GB of memory per core and a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, with no bundled local storage or GPU. Performance benchmarks show average single-core execution, as seen in Geekbench and stress-ng tests, but weaker multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth. The instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources without high parallel processing demands. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, lightweight web serving, text processing, and development environments.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg7.xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, suitable for lightweight web serving and development.

tagline: A dedicated four-core x86_64 compute instance with balanced memory allocation for general-purpose workloads and single-threaded application tasks.
